description: Doti workflow profile for building scaffold-dotnet and its first generated .NET CLI solution template.

selfHostingStatus.commandAvailabilityFootnote: Hygiene scanning, restore/build/test, the bootstrap CLI, the platform probe, the Doti renderer, the `dotnet new` template pack, `Hx.Scaffold.Cli new`/`version`/`release`/`prereq`, `doti install`/`doti payload check`/`doti install-hooks`, the architecture gate, `gate run`, the affected-test planner (`Hx.Impact.Cli plan`), GitVersion versioning (`version calculate`; release tags are created only by `hx release --major|--minor|--patch`), and the security gate (`security scan` — package-vulnerability SCA via `dotnet list package --vulnerable`, plus the build-integrated analyzer SAST status; enforced at release, advisory in dev) are all available. Operational `hx` commands require an executable-adjacent `hx.config.json`; `hx release` validates that Microsoft Configuration file before inspecting the target repo, then reads `.doti/release.json` so a vendored hx publishes the target repo's declared product executable instead of requiring `tools/Hx.Scaffold.Cli` in that repo. It validates the requested release intent, creates or verifies the local annotated GitVersion tag, stages vendored assets into the Velopack pack directory, runs `vpk pack`, records payload/package checks and config source in `LocalReleaseResult` and `release.identity.json`, and copies artifacts to the configured local release root when `localReleaseOutput.enabled` is true. Product executable updates are owned by Velopack installer/update artifacts; repo asset install/repair remains `doti install`; scaffold payload parity is enforced by `doti payload check` and the gate. The scaffold CLI uses a trusted prerequisite manifest for .NET SDK/Git/directory preflight; Windows automatic remediation is only through `hx prereq install` with release-defined winget package/source metadata and an exact operator-approved plan digest. The .NET security analyzers + Gitleaks are the SAST gate. Secret scanning is the vendored Gitleaks; Sentrux is pinned to the Heurex fork v0.5.11 for declared RIDs with matching grammars; GitVersion is vendored for win-x64 (large binary gitignored, an operational vendor step). The build is fully green on win-x64; other undeclared tool RIDs fail closed. The doti cycle-state substrate (`doti cycle stamp`/`status`: diff-bound stage proofs + freshness detection) and the single-sourced Operator-Question Protocol render into every skill + the agent context. `cycle stamp` now fails closed when a non-initial stage's prerequisites are missing, stale, or invalid, and when the first-stage feature slug is not numbered (`NNN-short-name`); when stamping release, `--release-intent` writes the matching GitVersion `+semver:` signal into the coded drift-review transition commit. The enforcing chokepoints are `doti cycle check` (fail-closed prerequisites), `doti question check` (the operator-question validator), and an untracked insurance pre-commit hook (`doti install-hooks`). Commits are owned by coded Doti workflow transitions and release paths, not by an agent-visible commit command. `new` and `doti install` auto-arm or refresh the Doti hook for Git repos and refuse to overwrite non-Doti pre-commit hooks. `gate run` persists a change-set-bound proof. Direct `dotnet test` remains useful for diagnosis but cannot satisfy transition or release proof.
selfHostingStatus.rootMaturityNote: `Hx.Scaffold.Cli new` is the single generation entry point and runs trusted prerequisite preflight before output mutation; operational `hx` commands require an executable-adjacent `hx.config.json`; `hx prereq check` reports .NET SDK/Git/directory readiness and `hx prereq install` can run only a Windows winget plan whose release-defined package/source metadata and manifest identity match an exact operator-approved digest. `hx release` is vendored-target-aware and Velopack-backed: it validates the executable-local Microsoft Configuration file, validates `.doti/release.json`, verifies the GitVersion-calculated major/minor/patch intent, creates or verifies the local annotated release tag, publishes the repo's declared executable, stages vendored assets into the Velopack pack directory, runs `vpk pack`, records the target, tag, payload checks, config source, and package artifacts in `LocalReleaseResult`/`release.identity.json`, and copies artifacts to the configured local release root when `localReleaseOutput.enabled` is true. `hx release` does not push tags; `/09-doti-release` owns the later remote push/CI verification. `doti cycle stamp --stage release --release-intent <major|minor|patch>` records the release intent in the automatic drift-review transition commit as a GitVersion `+semver:` signal before `hx release` validates the calculated version. `gate run --profile <auto|advisory|normal|release>` aggregates the deterministic ladder into one fail-closed `GateProof`; the proof includes canonical affected-test planner, test-scope, and execution hashes that Doti workflow transitions and release paths recompute before relying on them. The affected-test planner (`Hx.Impact.Cli plan`) deterministically scopes the gate's normal/advisory test run (project-graph reverse-closure -> covering test projects; fail-closed escalation); release runs the full suite. The self-hosted gate executes prebuilt test outputs after the command-backed build so it cannot overwrite its own loaded runner assemblies. Human help for all scaffold CLIs routes through one shared rich/plain renderer; `--help-mode plain`, `--plain-help`, `HX_HELP_MODE=plain`, and `NO_COLOR` provide ANSI-free help. Versioning is GitVersion-backed (`version calculate`; release tag creation lives in `hx release`). The security gate (`security scan`) runs package-vulnerability SCA (`dotnet list package --vulnerable`) + reports the build-integrated analyzer SAST status (CA3xxx/CA5xxx as errors — the build is the SAST enforcement point); enforced at release, advisory in dev. The .NET analyzers + Gitleaks are the SAST gate. Vendored tools (Gitleaks/Sentrux/GitVersion) are pinned + hashed; Sentrux is the Heurex fork v0.5.11 for declared RIDs with matching grammars, and large binaries are gitignored as an operational vendor step. The gate never creates a Sentrux baseline. Installed skills are rendered from one source — edit `.doti/core/skills.json` and re-render; do not hand-edit installed skill files. The doti cycle-state substrate (`doti cycle stamp`/`status`) records diff-bound stage proofs + freshness; `stamp` refuses a first-stage feature slug that is not numbered (`NNN-short-name`) and refuses non-initial stages until every transitive prerequisite is stamped + fresh. The enforcing chokepoints are `doti cycle check` (every transitive prerequisite stamped + fresh), `doti question check` (the operator-question validator), plus an untracked, logic-free insurance pre-commit hook (`doti install-hooks`) that blocks bare commits. `Hx.Scaffold.Cli new` and `doti install` auto-arm/refresh the hook for Git repos and fail hard rather than overwrite non-Doti hooks. `gate run` persists a change-set-bound proof. The workflow is command-enforced at the chokepoints, and commits are owned by coded Doti workflow transitions and release paths.
